WebValidate
  Validate API responses

Usage:
  WebValidate [options]

Options:
  -f, --files <files>                                List of files to test (required) [default: ]
  -s, --server <server>                              Server(s) to test (required) [default: ]
  -p, --port <port>                                  Port for web listener  (requires --run-loop) [default: 8080]
  --delay-start <delay-start>                        Delay test start (seconds) [default: 0]
  --duration <duration>                              Test duration (seconds)  (requires --run-loop) [default: 0]
  -g, --log-format <Json|JsonCamel|None|Tsv|TsvMin>  Log format [default: TsvMin]
  --max-errors <max-errors>                          Max validation errors [default: 10]
  --random                                           Run requests randomly (requires --run-loop) [default: False]
  --region <region>                                  Region deployed to (user defined) [default: ]
  -r, --run-loop                                     Run test in an infinite loop [default: False]
  -l, --sleep <sleep>                                Sleep (ms) between each request [default: 0]
  --tag <tag>                                        Tag for log (user defined) [default: ]
  -t, --timeout <timeout>                            Request timeout (seconds) [default: 30]
  -v, --verbose                                      Display all request results [default: False]
  --verbose-errors                                   Log verbose error messages [default: False]
  --summary <Json|JsonCamel|None|Tsv|Xml>            Display test summary (invalid with --run-loop) [default: None]
  --zone <zone>                                      Zone deployed to (user defined) [default: ]
  -u, --url-prefix <url-prefix>                      Url prefix for requests [default: ]
  -d, --dry-run                                      Validates configuration
  --version                                          Displays version and exits
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information
